analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-331

POL-331.title: IMPLEMENT: June 5 doctrine gaps — Medic, Charts, Navigation Before Retrieval, Route Health, Librarian drift
POL-331.body:
## Objective

Implement all architectural gaps identified between the feature branch `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` (built against June 4 spec) and the June 5 final decisions doctrine.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) supersedes the June 4 spec and introduces five new areas not covered by the feature branch:

1. **Medic role** — first-class durable role with dispatch flow and failed result packet handling
2. **Chart system** — `smartdocs/medic/charts/`, Chart ID format, schema, CLI command
3. **Navigation Before Retrieval** — doctrine in POLARIS_RULES.md + lint enforcement
4. **Route Health + Canonical References** — required SUMMARY.md sections
5. **Librarian drift reconciliation checklist** — formal post-run checklist in closeout-librarian skill

Gap analysis source: review of `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` diff against June 5 doctrine conducted 2026-06-05.

## Goal

All five doctrine gaps are implemented and validated. The feature branch and this cluster together bring the codebase into full alignment with the June 5 adoption architecture.

## Scope

* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/steps/04-execute-child.md
* .polaris/skills/closeout-librarian/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/closeout-librarian/steps/
* .polaris/skills/polaris-catalog/steps/03-reconcile-summary-md.md
* .polaris/skills/ROUTING.md
* src/medic/chart-id.ts
* src/medic/chart-schema.ts
* src/cli/medic.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/lint/map-reference-validator.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/summary-generator.ts
* src/types/result-packet.ts
* smartdocs/medic/charts/.gitkeep

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema) done: `polaris medic chart create` produces valid charts; schema validation passes
- [ ] [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet) done: Medic skill dispatachable; Foreman detects failed packets and routes to Medic
- [ ] [POL-329](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-329/update-summarymd-template-route-health-sections-and-canonical) done: SUMMARY.md scaffold includes Route Health and Canonical References
- [ ] [POL-328](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-328/add-navigation-before-retrieval-doctrine-to-polaris-rulesmd-and-lint) done: Generated POLARIS_RULES.md includes Navigation Before Retrieval section; lint flags broad preloads
- [ ] [POL-330](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-330/update-closeout-librarian-skill-formal-drift-reconciliation-checklist) done: closeout-librarian runs formal drift reconciliation checklist every run
- [ ] All tests pass: `npm run build && npm test`

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/medic src/cli/adopt-rules src/lint src/smartdocs-engine

## Ordering

Recommended execution order:

1. [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema) (Chart system — foundational, no dependencies)
2. [POL-329](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-329/update-summarymd-template-route-health-sections-and-canonical) (SUMMARY.md template — independent)
3. [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet) (Medic skill — depends on [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema))
4. [POL-328](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-328/add-navigation-before-retrieval-doctrine-to-polaris-rulesmd-and-lint) (Navigation Before Retrieval — depends on branch merge)
5. [POL-330](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-330/update-closeout-librarian-skill-formal-drift-reconciliation-checklist) (Librarian drift — depends on [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet), [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema), [POL-329](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-329/update-summarymd-template-route-health-sections-and-canonical))

## Non-goals

* Do not merge `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` as part of this cluster — branch merge is a prerequisite, not a child task
* Do not implement Sub-plan 2 (doctrine enforcement) or Sub-plan 3 (adoption UX) in this cluster
* Do not implement automated drift detection tooling — checklists are model-executed

POL-329.title: Update SUMMARY.md template: Route Health sections and Canonical References block
POL-329.body:
## Objective

Update the SUMMARY.md template in `polaris-catalog` and any cognition scaffold generators to include Route Health sections and a Canonical References block, per the June 5 doctrine.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) specifies that SUMMARY.md is the route situation report (shift handoff) and must contain:

* **Route Health** — Healthy / Monitoring / Known Issues / Recent Treatments / Improvement Opportunities
* **Canonical References** — `canonical_docs:` YAML block listing navigation paths (not reading assignments)

Current `polaris-catalog` step `03-reconcile-summary-md.md` does not reflect this structure, and any `src/` cognition scaffold generator does not template these sections.

## Goal

New SUMMARY.md files generated by polaris-catalog include Route Health skeleton and Canonical References block. Existing files are not destructively overwritten — reconcile updates only stale state sections.

## Scope

* .polaris/skills/polaris-catalog/steps/03-reconcile-summary-md.md
* src/smartdocs-engine/summary-generator.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/summary-generator.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris-catalog` step `03-reconcile-summary-md.md` documents the full required SUMMARY.md structure including Route Health and Canonical References
- [ ] Cognition scaffold generator (`summary-generator.ts` or equivalent) templates Route Health skeleton and `canonical_docs:` block on first creation
- [ ] Reconcile does not overwrite an existing Route Health section if it has content
- [ ] Canonical References block uses `canonical_docs:` key
- [ ] Route Health status is human-readable in under 10 seconds

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine

## Ordering

* No hard dependencies; can proceed independently of [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet)/327
* Should be done before Sub-plan 3 adoption UX work begins

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-populate Route Health status from runtime data — scaffold a skeleton only
* Do not change POLARIS.md templates (stable route map, separate from shift handoff)
* Do not modify existing SUMMARY.md files in the repo

POL-330.title: Update closeout-librarian skill: formal drift reconciliation checklist
POL-330.body:
## Objective

Update the `closeout-librarian` skill with a formal post-run drift reconciliation checklist covering worker results, Medic results, charts, route cognition, and drift observations.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) makes Librarian drift reconciliation a required step before finalize. "Implementation complete does not equal run complete." The current `closeout-librarian` skill does not have a structured checklist for this.

Drift observation types: `summary_outdated`, `canon_mismatch`, `route_guidance_outdated`, `missing_documentation`, `invalid_reference`.

After every completed run, Librarian must answer:

* Does SUMMARY.md still describe reality?
* Does POLARIS.md still describe the route?
* Did behavior, dependencies, or guidance change?
* Did a chart reveal reusable knowledge?

## Goal

The `closeout-librarian` skill explicitly runs a drift reconciliation checklist on every run. Chart linking in SUMMARY.md Recent Treatments is required when Medic ran. Drift observations are recorded if detected.

## Scope

* .polaris/skills/closeout-librarian/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/closeout-librarian/steps/

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `closeout-librarian` chain includes a formal drift reconciliation step
- [ ] Checklist covers: worker results, Medic results (if any), charts created, POLARIS.md, SUMMARY.md, canonical references, repo changes
- [ ] Chart linking in SUMMARY.md `## Recent Treatments` is a required action when Medic ran this run
- [ ] Drift observations are recorded when detected (in chart if Medic ran, otherwise noted in SUMMARY.md)
- [ ] Drift observation type vocabulary is documented in the skill

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet) (Medic role) and [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema) (Chart system) being done first — drift checklist references chart artifacts
* Depends on [POL-329](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-329/update-summarymd-template-route-health-sections-and-canonical) (SUMMARY.md Route Health template) — Recent Treatments section must exist

## Non-goals

* Do not implement automated drift detection tooling — this is a model-executed checklist
* Do not resolve drift inline during Librarian — record observations; resolution is a separate task if needed

POL-327.title: Implement Chart system: smartdocs/medic/charts/, chart ID generator, schema, and CLI command
POL-327.body:
## Objective

Implement the Chart system: folder structure, Chart ID generator, schema validation, and `polaris medic chart create` CLI command.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) defines Charts as the Medic's primary output artifact. The repository is treated as a patient; Charts are medical records. No implementation exists.

Chart ID format: `CHART-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN` (zero-padded sequence per day).
Chart folder: `smartdocs/medic/charts/` — nothing enters without a valid Chart ID.

Required front-matter: `chart_id`, `cluster_id`, `route`, `status`, `related_charts`, `created`, `updated`, `drift_observations` (optional).
Required sections: Problem, Symptoms, Root Cause, Affected Files, Treatment, Validation, Prevention, When To Read This Chart.
Supported relationship types: `same_failure`, `edge_case_of`, `regression_of`, `caused_by`, `fixed_by`, `supersedes`, `duplicate_of`.

## Goal

`polaris medic chart create` scaffolds a valid chart. Schema validation rejects charts missing required metadata or sections. Chart relationships are parseable by the runtime.

## Scope

* src/medic/chart-id.ts
* src/medic/chart-schema.ts
* src/medic/chart-id.test.ts
* src/medic/chart-schema.test.ts
* src/cli/medic.ts
* smartdocs/medic/charts/.gitkeep

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris medic chart create` produces a chart with a valid `CHART-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN` ID
- [ ] Daily sequence resets per day and is zero-padded to 3 digits
- [ ] Schema validation rejects charts missing required front-matter fields
- [ ] Schema validation rejects charts missing any required section heading
- [ ] Chart relationships can be declared and are parseable
- [ ] `smartdocs/medic/charts/` folder exists in the repo

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/medic

## Ordering

* No dependencies — this is foundational for [POL-326](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-326/implement-medic-role-skill-dispatch-flow-and-failed-result-packet) (Medic skill) and [POL-330](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-330/update-closeout-librarian-skill-formal-drift-reconciliation-checklist) (Librarian drift)

## Non-goals

* Do not implement chart promotion/archival logic (that belongs to Librarian, [POL-330](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-330/update-closeout-librarian-skill-formal-drift-reconciliation-checklist))
* Do not integrate charts into docs-ingest pipeline in this issue

POL-326.title: Implement Medic role: skill, dispatch flow, and failed result packet handling
POL-326.body:
## Objective

Implement the Medic role as a first-class durable role: create the `polaris-medic` skill, update Foreman dispatch logic to handle failed result packets, and define the failed result packet schema.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) establishes Medic as a durable role alongside Analyst, Foreman, Worker, and Librarian. The feature branch `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` was built against the June 4 spec and does not include this role. Medic handles exceptions — it does not perform normal implementation.

Dispatch flow per doctrine:

```
Worker → failure → failed result packet
  → Foreman marks triage required
  → Foreman continues runnable work
  → Medic dispatched → diagnosis → repair → validation → chart creation
  → Librarian reconciliation → finalize
```

## Goal

Medic is a fully dispatachable skill. Foreman can detect a failed result packet and dispatch Medic before Librarian runs. Runnable sibling work continues in parallel.

## Scope

* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/
* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/SKILL.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-medic/steps/
* .polaris/skills/ROUTING.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/steps/04-execute-child.md
* src/types/result-packet.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.polaris/skills/polaris-medic/` skill exists with SKILL.md, chain.md, and steps: orient-medic, diagnose, repair, validate, create-chart, closeout
- [ ] ROUTING.md includes `polaris-medic` route
- [ ] `polaris-run` step `04-execute-child.md` detects failed result packets and marks triage required
- [ ] Foreman dispatches Medic before Librarian when triage is required
- [ ] Runnable sibling work continues while Medic is dispatched
- [ ] Failed result packet schema is defined

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-327](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-327/implement-chart-system-smartdocsmediccharts-chart-id-generator-schema) (Chart system) — Medic's create-chart step requires chart schema and ID generator
* [POL-330](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-330/update-closeout-librarian-skill-formal-drift-reconciliation-checklist) (Librarian drift checklist) should follow this

## Non-goals

* Do not implement actual diagnosis or repair AI logic — skill steps define the protocol, not the intelligence
* Do not change worker execution logic beyond failed packet detection

POL-328.title: Add Navigation Before Retrieval Doctrine to POLARIS_RULES.md and lint enforcement
POL-328.body:
## Objective

Add Navigation Before Retrieval Doctrine to the generated `POLARIS_RULES.md` template and extend the map-reference lint validator to flag broad-preload instructions in skill chains.

## Context

The June 5 doctrine (`smartdocs/doctrine/active/2026-06-05-adoption-architecture-final-decisions.md`) establishes Navigation Before Retrieval as a core principle: links are retrieval paths, not reading assignments. Workers must not speculatively preload linked docs, all doctrine, or all charts.

The feature branch `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` adds `POLARIS_RULES.md` generation (`src/cli/adopt-rules.ts`) and a map-reference lint validator, but neither captures this doctrine.

## Goal

`POLARIS_RULES.md` generated by `polaris init --adopt` includes a Navigation Before Retrieval section. The lint validator catches broad-preload instructions in skill chain files.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* src/lint/map-reference-validator.ts
* src/lint/map-reference-validator.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generated `POLARIS_RULES.md` includes a `## Navigation Before Retrieval` section with the doctrine text and expected 5-step behavior
- [ ] Lint validator flags skill chain files containing instructions to "read all doctrine", "load all charts", or "preload" linked docs
- [ ] Lint violation message: `[navigation-before-retrieval] Skill chain instructs broad context preload — use targeted retrieval instead`
- [ ] Existing map-reference lint tests continue to pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules src/lint

## Ordering

* Depends on `feat/adoption-command-surface-agent-files` being merged first (adds adopt-rules.ts and lint validator)

## Non-goals

* Do not enforce Navigation Before Retrieval at runtime execution — this issue covers generation and static lint only
* Do not modify existing POLARIS_RULES.md in the repo root (only the generator template)
